html:
Eight Months In: A Round-Up of Recent Developments

Replication Research (R2) continues to grow, and we're pleased to share several updates from the past few weeks.

First peer review report, decisions, and PubPeer posts
Our first peer review report is now publicly available on Zenodo, and the first formal editorial decisions have been made. In line with our article review procedure, we have also posted our first decisions including reviewer comments on PubPeer. Transparent peer review is a core part of R2's identity, and making reports and decisions openly accessible is one of the ways we put that into practice.

R2 in the Open Economics Guide
The ZBW's Open Economics Guide published an interview with R2's journal manager and editors, covering the state of replication research in economics and business studies, and offering practical entry points for researchers considering their first replication study. Read it here: R2: A New Home for Replication Research – Plus Tips for Getting Started

Featured in RoRI's Go Meta! blog
The Research on Research Institute (RoRI) featured replications and reproductions including a mention of R2 in their Go Meta! newsletter. Their post, "Did you know this study was replicated?", explores how replication findings are tracked and disseminated. We're glad to see growing attention to this question in the broader research community.

Replication Hub Showcase – July 1st
R2 will take part in the upcoming Replication Hub Showcase on July 1st, which will also feature the Replication Journal Federation. If you'd like to attend, register here until July 1st, 11 am CEST: Replication Hub Showcase registration

The founding story of R2 — Metascience Matters podcast
How did R2 come about, and how is it funded? Journal manager Lukas Röseler tells the story in an episode of the Metascience Matters podcast: Metascience Matters, timestamp 13:43

Website updates
We have been making a number of small tweaks and improvements to the journal's website design - more will follow.
title: Updates from Replication Research: First Decisions, Press Coverage, and Upcoming Events

html:
We have several exciting updates and new initiatives to share with the Replication Research community as we continue our mission to support and publish high-quality replication studies.

Submissions Update: We are excited to report that our inaugural call for submissions is going well. We have received fantastic engagement from the community, and our first wave of manuscript submissions is currently under peer review. Thank you to our submitting authors and our dedicated reviewers. If you have a replication study in the works, we encourage you to submit or send a pre-submission inquiry!

Launch of the Mentorship Program: We have officially launched the Replication Research Mentorship Program, which engages students and postdocs as volunteers in a variety of journal tasks, offering them valuable editorial experience and academic self-governance skills.

Depending on their past experience, mentees contribute to typesetting and reprochecks, or participate directly in the editorial workflow as reviewers and associate editors. To ensure proper recognition, all contributions are transparently credited on our editorial website and within manuscript metadata. Send an email to contact@replicationresearch.org if you are interested.

Replication Journal Federation (RJF) Launch: In our ongoing effort to promote robust, transparent scientific practices, we are proud to be a part of the newly launched Replication Journal Federation (RJF). The RJF is a collaborative network aimed at streamlining the publication of replications and elevating the status of replication research across disciplines. Check out the RJF members here.

New Role: Science Communication Champion: To increase the accessibility and real-world impact of the research we publish, we have officially updated the Replication Research constitution to include a brand-new editorial role: the Science Communication Champion. The primary task of this role is to provide clear, accessible lay summaries of our published articles in coordination with the authors. At the R2 General Assembly, we have elected Helena Hartmann to be our SCC. She will ensure that the reports can be understood and utilized by researchers from other disciplines, the broader public, practitioners, and policymakers.

You can find more detailed information about the state of R2 in the slides from April's R2 General Assembly at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19726752. 

Thank you for your continued support of Replication Research. We look forward to sharing more updates and our first published papers with you soon!
title: Updates from Replication Research: Mentorship Program, RJF Launch, and Submissions Update

html:
Love Replications Week: Registration is now open for the Love Replications Week. Among many other contributions, there will be a dedicated Replication Journal Showcase where the journals Rescience C, Rescience X, the Journal of Comments and Replications in Economics, the Journal of Robustness Reports, the Journal of Open Psychology Data, and Replication Research will be presented.

Partnerships: MetaROR provides a platform that leverages the publish-review-curate (PRC) model. We are now a partner of MetaROR. This means that, similarly to PCI recommendations, we commit to using MetaROR review reports to offer a streamlined experience to authors. R2 is also a partner of the curated data repository Tromso Repository of Language and Linguistics (TROLLing), which we recommend for authors who submit research from the field of linguistics, and the Journal of Comments and Replications in Economics (JCRe), which, unlike us, publishes replications from the field of economics and has several years of experience in diamond open access publishing. All partners are listed in our website's sidebar.

Initial Editorial Assessment: After having already received multiple submissions, we have documented and published the procedure that all submissions undergo before peer-review. This Initial Editorial Assessment is a combination of manual and automated checks and it is free for other researchers or journals to re-use or change in accordance with their needs: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17911972
title: Love Replications Week, Partnerships, and Initial Editorial Assessment

html:
PCI Collaborations
After having become a category 2 PCI-friendly journal for PCI Psychology, PCI Neuroscience, and PCI Organization Studies in November, R2 is now also a category 2 PCI-friendly journal for PCI Health & Movement Sciences and a PCI-friendly journal for PCI Registered Reports. Specific requirements for PCI-RR recommended articles to be published at R2 are listed here.

Save the date: Love Replications Week
In the Love Replications Week, you can learn how to do reproduction and replication studies yourself, submit your own local or virtual events, and show your love for replications — regardless of the type of research that you do! There will be daily events in the week from March 2 - 6.
title: More PCI collaborations and Save the Date for Love Replications Week

html:
What is PCI?

PCI stands for Peer Community in and is a free recommendation process of scientific preprints. Once a preprint is recommended, authors can choose to publish their article with (PCI friendly category 2 or PCI interested) or without (PCI friendly category 1) further peer review in participating journals. PCI allows for transparent quality assurance of scientific articles and reverses competition in that journals and not authors compete against each other.

How are PCI-recommended preprints treated?

Specifically, there is the option for PCI-recommended preprints to be accepted without further peer-review. As category 2 PCI-friendly journal, we will inform authors within five days about our decision:

  • Acceptance with minor modifications and with no further peer-review.
  • Need for further peer-review or other checks before decision.
  • Not interested.

Recommended preprints need to meet R2's scope (e.g., be replications, reproductions, or related contributions) and - as all articles - will undergo a reproducibility check and optionally a social responsibility evaluation prior to publication. Specifically, we are a category 2 PCI-friendly journal for PCI Psychology, PCI Neuroscience, and PCI Organization Studies. In case of acceptance, published articles will include an editors' note that they were reviewed by PCI. In the near future, we also plan to become PCI-friendly for PCI-RR.
title: R2 is now PCI-friendly

html: Replication Research is now open for submissions. Alongside the journal launch, we have published the journal’s constitution, website materials, a stopping rule, and our Handbook for Reproduction and Replication Studies. Accompanying materials for the journal have been published in the R2 Zenodo Community at zenodo.org/communities/r2/records.
title: R2 is now open for submissions

html:
R2 has been created by researchers and for researchers. To align it to researchers' and convictions as strongly as possible, we are launching the Open Community Call. We invite researchers to share their thoughts and comments on our journal materials between now and September 5. In the remaining weeks ahead of the launch on October 10, we will engage with the feedback and finalize the journal concept.

Contribute here: Open Community Call
title: Open Community Call

html: During the Replication Research Symposium, we have revealed that researchers will be able to submit their manuscript to Replication Research from 10 October 2025.
title: R2 will be open for submissions from 10-10-2025!
This website is in development. Submissions are not open until fall 2025.
